content:
I'm wondering if the following changes would be worth submitting as a patch:

  1. core::ffi::c_void in rust/kernel/kunit.rs
use core::{ffi::c_void, fmt};
// ...
#[doc(hidden)]
pub fn err(args: fmt::Arguments<'_>) {
    // SAFETY: The format string is null-terminated and the `%pA` specifier matches the argument we
    // are passing.
    #[cfg(CONFIG_PRINTK)]
    unsafe {
        bindings::_printk(
            c"\x013%pA".as_ptr() as _,
            &args as *const _ as *const c_void,
        );
    }
}
// ...
#[doc(hidden)]
pub fn info(args: fmt::Arguments<'_>) {
    // SAFETY: The format string is null-terminated and the `%pA` specifier matches the argument we
    // are passing.
    #[cfg(CONFIG_PRINTK)]
    unsafe {
        bindings::_printk(
            c"\x016%pA".as_ptr() as _,
            &args as *const _ as *const c_void,
        );
    }
}

Ideally, crate::ffi::c_void would be used, but it's just a simple wrapper around core::ffi::c_void. They're equivalent for now, so it doesn't make a big difference, but this might help with consistency and would account for any future changes (well, it seems to be pretty unlikely that c_void would ever become anything other than core::ffi::c_void).

I believe use core::ffi::c_void can be simply removed, because commit 3d5bef5d47c371e1704 ("rust: add C FFI types to the prelude") made all the FFI types available through the prelude.

  1. doctest in rust/kernel/init.rs
//! ```rust,ignore
//! # #![allow(unreachable_pub, clippy::disallowed_names)]
//! use kernel::{prelude::*, types::Opaque};
//! use core::{ptr::addr_of_mut, marker::PhantomPinned, pin::Pin};
//! # mod bindings {
//! #     #![allow(non_camel_case_types)]
//! #     pub struct foo;
//! #     pub unsafe fn init_foo(_ptr: *mut foo) {}
//! #     pub unsafe fn destroy_foo(_ptr: *mut foo) {}
//! #     pub unsafe fn enable_foo(_ptr: *mut foo, _flags: u32) -> i32 { 0 }
//! # }
//! # // `Error::from_errno` is `pub(crate)` in the `kernel` crate, thus provide a workaround.
//! # trait FromErrno {
//! #     fn from_errno(errno: core::ffi::c_int) -> Error {
//! #         // Dummy error that can be constructed outside the `kernel` crate.
//! #         Error::from(core::fmt::Error)
//! #     }

Similarly, in this doctest, it might make sense to use c_int instead of core::ffi::c_int in the signature fn from_errno(errno: core::ffi::c_int) -> Error. That said, this doctest is marked with ignore attribute, so the practical impact is minimal.

both sound sensible to me

The FromErrno trait in 2. seems a bit weird, maybe that is a workaround that's no longer needed? cc @Miguel Ojeda

It is a workaround I added to be able to build those tests cleanly (commit a30e94c29673 ("rust: init: make doctests compilable/testable")), which is not needed anymore as you say since we made those public (commit 5ed147473458 ("rust: error: make conversion functions public")).

However, the tests are ignore (commit 206dea39e559 ("rust: init: disable doctests")), but I don't think they need to be, e.g. we can do some cleanups here to build them again:

diff --git a/rust/kernel/init.rs b/rust/kernel/init.rs
index 8d228c237954..e57a30a5d20c 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/init.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/init.rs
@@ -29,15 +29,15 @@
 //!
 //! ## General Examples
 //!
-//! ```rust,ignore
-//! # #![allow(clippy::disallowed_names)]
+//! ```rust
+//! # #![expect(clippy::disallowed_names, clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks)]
 //! use kernel::types::Opaque;
 //! use pin_init::pin_init_from_closure;
 //!
 //! // assume we have some `raw_foo` type in C:
 //! #[repr(C)]
 //! struct RawFoo([u8; 16]);
-//! extern {
+//! extern "C" {
 //!     fn init_foo(_: *mut RawFoo);
 //! }
 //!
@@ -66,25 +66,17 @@
 //! });
 //! ```
 //!
-//! ```rust,ignore
-//! # #![allow(unreachable_pub, clippy::disallowed_names)]
+//! ```rust
+//! # #![expect(unreachable_pub, clippy::disallowed_names)]
 //! use kernel::{prelude::*, types::Opaque};
 //! use core::{ptr::addr_of_mut, marker::PhantomPinned, pin::Pin};
 //! # mod bindings {
-//! #     #![allow(non_camel_case_types)]
+//! #     #![expect(non_camel_case_types, clippy::missing_safety_doc)]
 //! #     pub struct foo;
 //! #     pub unsafe fn init_foo(_ptr: *mut foo) {}
 //! #     pub unsafe fn destroy_foo(_ptr: *mut foo) {}
 //! #     pub unsafe fn enable_foo(_ptr: *mut foo, _flags: u32) -> i32 { 0 }
 //! # }
-//! # // `Error::from_errno` is `pub(crate)` in the `kernel` crate, thus provide a workaround.
-//! # trait FromErrno {
-//! #     fn from_errno(errno: core::ffi::c_int) -> Error {
-//! #         // Dummy error that can be constructed outside the `kernel` crate.
-//! #         Error::from(core::fmt::Error)
-//! #     }
-//! # }
-//! # impl FromErrno for Error {}
 //! /// # Invariants
 //! ///
 //! /// `foo` is always initialized

There are other things that apparently got lost, like the expect use and the missing_safety_doc one, at 86f7dacadeec ("rust: add extensions to the pin-init crate and move relevant documentation there").
yeah that looks good
I'll just send a patch that handles only 1., then. Thanks for reviewing!
feel free to also send the stuff that Miguel said for 2
you'll need to credit him correctly though
Ah, got it. Then I'll take both of them.
If you don't mind, I can clean that up, since the history is a bit convoluted and I wanted to note those commits in the log.
(I mean the diff above)
Sure, that'd be great!

Something like: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250526152914.2453949-1-ojeda@kernel.org/

There are other cleanups that could be done, like writing SAFETY comments or similar, in case you want new things to do around that code since you already took a look (sorry that the c_* one you suggested is not needed when that code goes away...)
Another one you can do is use &raw mut to clean the addr_of_mut
Ah, no, Antonio did that one for the doctests too already: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250316061429.817126-3-contact@antoniohickey.com/#Z31rust:kernel:init.rs
I've just sent a patch for the first one! (It took some time since I double-checked the r4l documentations)
Replied :)

Miguel Ojeda said:

Something like: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250526152914.2453949-1-ojeda@kernel.org/

There are other cleanups that could be done, like writing SAFETY comments or similar, in case you want new things to do around that code since you already took a look (sorry that the c_* one you suggested is not needed when that code goes away...)

No worries at all, I actually stumbled across that while casually browsing the code out of interest. Appreciate you taking the time to look for something else!

It took some time since I double-checked the r4l documentations

It is great to take time to read the docs! Not everyone does that, so thanks!

I'll change my commit message according to your reply.

I wonder if I need to resend w/ [Patch v2] even if the diff is same...

If you resend, yeah, please use v2 even if no content changed (otherwise it can get confusing for humans and tooling).

But I would suggest waiting a few days for more feedback (and possibly for the chance to collect tags from reviewers and testers). And, sometimes, for small changes like that, maintainers may be willing to fix the log for you when they apply it (though normally new versions that can be applied cleanly make their life easier and thus are usually welcome :)
Ok, I'll apply all the reviewers' suggestions at once. Thanks!
